behaviors.description: The generated reply references specific points from the incoming email rather than producing a generic response.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit < Integration because the failure boundary is the handoff from thread ingestion/prompt construction to generation, not a single pure function. Integration < System because a real model/API path can introduce runtime effects. System < Agentic because the core pass/fail depends on model judgment about relevance/specificity (LLM-DEP), and mocks would hide generic-but-plausible outputs. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets one generation quality decision, not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Use real incoming threads and a real LLM call; assert replies include concrete thread-specific references (e.g., named entities, prior commitments, quoted points) and fail when output is generic.

behaviors.description: The factual consistency check flags claims in the draft that contradict entries in the knowledge base.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit < Integration because contradiction detection requires module interaction (claim extraction/checker + KB lookup). Integration > System because this behavior can be caught with in-process modules and a local KB fixture; no real OS/network/subprocess boundary is required. Integration > Agentic because behavior text is deterministic against KB entries ("contradict entries in the knowledge base") and does not require model-quality judgment. Rejected higher levels based on that text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire checker + KB repository with seeded contradictory/non-contradictory cases; verify contradiction flags propagate correctly and include the offending claim references.

behaviors.description: When the knowledge base has no relevant entries, the draft includes a disclaimer rather than fabricating facts.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit < Integration because retrieval-result state must be handed to draft generation. Integration < System because real model/API execution path can matter. System < Agentic because correctness is about generation behavior under uncertainty (disclaimer vs fabrication), which depends on real LLM output and is hidden by mocks. Rejected Workflow: behavior text concerns one generation decision conditioned on empty KB, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run with real LLM and empty/irrelevant KB retrieval; assert draft contains required uncertainty disclaimer and does not introduce unsupported factual claims.

behaviors.description: The approval workflow blocks sending until the user explicitly approves — no draft is sent automatically.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit < Integration because the failure is at orchestration/state handoff (draft state, approval gate, sender invocation). Integration > System because a local in-process sender stub can expose premature-send bugs without real SMTP/API. Integration > Agentic because no LLM output quality is needed for this gate logic. Rejected System/Agentic due behavior text being explicit workflow gating logic.
behaviors.test_description: Exercise draft-created and unapproved states through approval service + send dispatcher; assert zero send calls before approval and exactly one send after explicit approval event.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — read thread, generate draft, check facts, present for approval — works end-to-end for a real email thread.
behaviors.justification: Pairwise tournament: Unit < Integration because many modules and handoffs are involved. Integration < System because behavior explicitly requires a real email thread/end-to-end environment. System < Agentic because sequence includes real LLM draft generation where output affects downstream checks. Agentic < Workflow because this is the full pipeline behavior with sequential stages and agentic decision(s) across the chain (MULTI-STEP/full agent pipeline). No concrete disqualifying text supports demotion.
behaviors.test_description: Execute end-to-end against a real email thread and real model path: ingest thread, generate draft, run fact check, gate on approval, and verify stage outputs and transitions across the entire pipeline.
